rulings.Case Title: ROBERT NELSON et al VS. AIR & LIQUID SYSTEMS CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, April 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. PLAINTIFFS ROBERT NELSON and CYNTHIA NELSON's DEMURRER TO Crane Co.'s ANSWER TO COMPLAINT is off calendar, The Plaintiff passed away on April 7, 2010. = (220/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT GOTTSCHALL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, April 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant John Crane, Inc.¿s motion is strike Plaintiff¿s complaint is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff was not served with notice 18 court days before the hearing. Because Plaintiff was never under any obligation to respond on the merits to the original improperly noticed motion, Defendant¿s amended notice of motion restarts the required 18 day notice period. See Robinson v. Woods (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1258. The court lacks discretion to continue the hearing date to cure defective notice unless Plaintiff consents. Ibid.; Urshan v. Musicians' Credit Union (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 758. Leave is given to re-file the motion with appropriate notice. =(220/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT GOTTSCHALL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, April 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant John Crane, Inc.¿s demurrer is overruled without prejudice. Plaintiff was not served with notice 18 court days before the hearing. Because Plaintiff was never under any obligation to respond on the merits to the original improperly noticed demurrer, Defendant¿s amended notice of demurrer restarts the required 18 day notice period. See Robinson v. Woods (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1258. The court lacks discretion to shorten the notice period or continue the hearing date to cure defective notice unless Plaintiff consents. Ibid.; Urshan v. Musicians' Credit Union (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 758. Leave is given to re-file the demurrer with appropriate notice. =(220/AJR)
